hypothesis: If pre-trained single-gas neural networks are used as feature extractors within a graph neural network architecture that encodes gas interactions as edges between nodes, then the resulting model will more accurately predict mixture etching rates under extrapolative conditions (unseen process parameter ranges and gas compositions) compared to a standard feedforward neural network trained directly on mixture data, because the graph structure explicitly captures pairwise gas interactions while the pre-trained components encode transferable single-gas plasma physics knowledge.
